item.title: AliExpress Has Kirby Air Riders for Nintendo Switch 2 for Less Than $40 With Free Delivery
item.content:encoded:
Switch 2 owners, check out this super low price on a first party Switch 2 exclusive game. Kirby Air Riders, which is normally $69.99 and has yet to be seen on sale for less than $65, can be yours for just $39.37 with free delivery after coupon code: "USAS06". This is an import edition, but it will play fine on any US-based console since all Switch 2 games are region-free/unlocked. According to the page, tariff fees are already included as long as you choose "AliExpress Standard Shipping". Don't choose another service like UPS or FedEx or you'll be dinged with import fees on delivery.

Kirby Air Riders (Nintendo Switch 2) for $39.37

Kirby Air Riders (Switch 2)

On the surface, Kirby Air Riders is a kart racer similar to Mario Kart World but with a different cast of characters including Kirby, Waddle Dee, King Dedede, and Meta Knight. However, they're actually quite different. While both are racing games, Mario Kart World focuses more on the racing mechanics whereas Kirby Air Riders puts more focus on the battle mechanics. Kirby Air Riders has a dedicated single player mode with an actual story called Road Trip. It also has a really fun City Trial mode where you get to explore a big city to collect power-ups and customize your vehicle across a total of 150 different challenges. My opinion is that you should own both games.

Eric Song is the IGN commerce manager in charge of finding the best gaming and tech deals every day. When Eric isn't hunting for deals for other people at work, he's hunting for deals for himself during his free time.



https://www.ign.com/articles/kirby-air-riders-for-nintendo-switch-2-deal-aliexpress-spring-sale
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Magic: The Gathering's Next Set is Getting a Special Tie-in Novel, and It's Already Discounted at Amazon
item.content:encoded:
Magic: The Gathering's Next Set is Getting a Special Tie-in Novel, and It's Already Discounted at Amazon

Omens of Chaos is almost here.

Magic: The Gathering’s Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle set feels like it just came out (because, really, it did), and we’re already getting excited about Secrets of Strixhaven.

This return to one of Magic’s classic planes will launch on April 24, but you can get deep into the lore sooner than that with a tie-in novel called Strixhaven: Omens of Chaos. Better yet, the first edition is seeing a preorder discount at Amazon, bringing it from $29 to $20.99.

Strixhaven: Omens of Chaos

The new novel comes from Seanan McGuire, who has previously worked on short fiction stories for Wizards of the Coast, and brings her history with the game to this dark academia novel.

You can check out the full synopsis below:

“Eula Blue was supposed to be a shield mage. That was before the war came—before the fight for the Multiverse devastated Eula’s home, and with it her hopes for a magical education.”

“But the destruction of the war also brought something new: the ability to travel to other planes. And when Eula receives an invitation to study magic at a distant school called Strixhaven, she leaps to take it.”

“Eula’s journey brings her closer than she ever thought possible to her fellow students, including the mysterious Segante, whose cutthroat home plane has left him slow to trust. But not everyone is thrilled by the arrival of the new class, and Eula and her new friends quickly become targets.”

“To make it through their first semester, they’ll have to fight for their place in this new world—or else they’ll be dead before their final exams.”

The first edition hardcover even includes a brand new Strixhaven version of Command Tower, which is certainly welcome. Interested in Magic lore? Let us know!

For more on Magic: The Gathering, check out why one TMNT card combo might be the fastest way to ruin your friendships.

Lloyd Coombes is an experienced freelancer in tech, gaming and fitness seen at Polygon, Eurogamer, Macworld, TechRadar and many more. He's a big fan of Magic: The Gathering and other collectible card games, much to his wife's dismay.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/magic-the-gatherings-secrets-of-strixhaven-omens-of-chaos-special-tie-in-novel-discounted-at-amazon
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D Processor Drops to the Lowest Price Ever, Also Includes Crimson Desert
item.content:encoded:
Get in on this deal ahead of the Amazon Spring Sale.

If you're in the process of building a new gaming PC, the AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D should be your top CPU pick, especially since it's discounted during the Amazon Spring Sale. For a limited time, this CPU has dropped to $419.95 with free shipping, which is the lowest price I've seen since its launch. Even better, it comes with a free voucher code for the highly anticipated Crimson Desert game, which will be released on March 19. The AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D is widely considered to be one of the best gaming processors on the market and outperforms even pricier AMD and Intel CPUs.

The Gamer's Choice: AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU for $419.95

Includes Crimson Desert

Free Crimson Desert with Purchase

AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D AM5 Desktop Processor

The AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D boasts a max boost clock of 5.2GHz with 8 cores, 16 threads, and 104MB of L2-L3 cache. What really makes it excel in games, however, is the 3D-V-Cache technology that's only found in AMD's X3D lineup. Despite being the least expensive processor in this stack, the 9800X3D performs nearly identically in games compared to its more expensive siblings. It has the fewest number of cores, but that doesn't matter for gaming. On Passmark, the 9800X3D has the second highest gaming score, trailing a mere 300 points behind the considerably pricier 9900X3D.

Note that there's a new 9850X3D that's now available. We reviewed it and, short answer, you're not missing out.

You're getting Crimson Desert for free

Crimson Desert is an open-world RPG that was released on March 19. It has received polarizing reviews, with the general consensus being that this is an epic game with a massive world, gorgeous visuals, and a plethora of content marred by bugs, glitches, quality of life issues, and an insipid story and characters. I myself have been playing this game and I am really enjoying it despite its flaws. More importantly, my system is equipped with a 9800X3D CPU and an Nvidia GPU. After over 20 hours of playtime, I've experienced stable performance and none of the crashes noted in our IGN review. In any case, you're essentially getting this game for free with this deal, so you have nothing to lose.

Eric Song is the IGN commerce manager in charge of finding the best gaming and tech deals every day. When Eric isn't hunting for deals for other people at work, he's hunting for deals for himself during his free time.

Recommends



https://www.ign.com/articles/amd-ryzen-7-9800x3d-cpu-deal-early-amazon-spring-sale
